title: Phalangium revolutum
taxonomy: Phalangium revolutum is classified in the family Phalangiidae and the order Opiliones, which is a group of arachnids commonly known as harvestmen or daddy long legs. This classification places Phalangium revolutum within the larger arachnid group. The order Opiliones is further divided into several suborders, with Phalangiidae being part of the suborder Eupnoi. This classification system helps scientists understand the evolutionary relationships among different species of harvestmen. Understanding the classification of Phalangium revolutum provides valuable insights into its biology and behavior.

The health benefits of Phalangium revolutum include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various skin conditions, wounds, and gastrointestinal issues.
